summary: Linked Jigglypuff/Purin `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` as a 936-byte matching C object by splitting the previous combined SpecialLw/SpecialS range. The PR carved `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` down to `0x8013CE8C..0x8013D234`, moved the remaining nonmatching Purin special code into `ftPr_SpecialS.c`/`ftPr_SpecialS.s`, updated build object lists and splits, and replaced several local float extern references with literals so the Lw file owns only its small `sdata2` constants. No review comments were present.
what_changed: `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` became a matching C object for the first 936 bytes of the old Purin special range, covering the PR report's 12 functions at 100%: `ftPr_SpecialLw_Enter`, `ftPr_SpecialAirLw_Enter`, Lw/AirLw Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll, and transition helpers `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D104` and `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D19C`.

The old assembly file `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialLw.s` was renamed to `ftPr_SpecialS.s` and had the now-linked Lw assembly removed; its `.file` changed from `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` to `ftPr_SpecialS.c`, and its text range now begins at `0x8013D234`.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` split the Purin range: `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` now has `.text start:0x8013CE8C end:0x8013D234` plus `.sdata2 start:0x804D9C20 end:0x804D9C2C`, while new `ftPr_SpecialS.c` owns `.text start:0x8013D234 end:0x801422E8`, `.sdata`, and the later `.sdata2 start:0x804D9C30 end:0x804D9C98`.
`configure.py` changed `melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialLw.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` and added `melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialS.c` as `NonMatching`.
`obj_files.mk` replaced the old Purin SpecialLw assembly object with the new C object and added the remaining `ftPr_SpecialS.s.o` assembly object.
`ftPr_SpecialS.c` and `ftPr_SpecialS.h` were added to hold declarations/code for the remaining SpecialS and later Purin special routines that had previously been grouped under SpecialLw.
`ftPr_SpecialLw.h` was pruned from a broad mixed-special header down to only the Lw-related prototypes.
`ftPr_Init.c` and `ftdata.c` now include `ftPr_SpecialS.h`; `ftPr_Init.c` also switched from including `ftPurin/types.h` to `types.h` in the shown diff.

In `ftPr_SpecialLw.c`, float externs `ftPr_Init_804D9C20`, `ftPr_Init_804D9C24`, and `ftPr_Init_804D9C28` were removed from the C source and call sites were rewritten using literal `-1`, `0`, and `1` where appropriate.
smart_moves: The PR did not try to link the entire old `ftPr_SpecialLw` assembly range at once; it isolated the first matching 936 bytes and renamed/split the remainder into `ftPr_SpecialS`, reducing scope while preserving the rest of the build.

Splitting `.sdata2` at `0x804D9C2C/0x804D9C30` kept the constants associated with the newly matching Lw file separate from later Purin special constants, which is important for object boundary accuracy.
Replacing small float constants with direct literals in the matching Lw source avoided unnecessary extern dependencies and aligned the generated code/data ownership with the new split.
The build system was updated in both `configure.py` and `obj_files.mk`, ensuring the newly linked C file is treated as `Matching` while the unlinked remainder remains assembled.
Header cleanup followed the file split: Lw declarations stayed in `ftPr_SpecialLw.h`, while SpecialS declarations moved into a new `ftPr_SpecialS.h`, making include dependencies less misleading.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly file covers multiple logical move files, a successful link may require carving the exact text/data boundaries in `splits.txt` and moving the remaining functions to a new C/asm pair rather than forcing one source file to own too much.
For small fighter move functions, preserving object-local constant ownership can matter as much as matching the text; watch `.sdata2` ranges when replacing extern float symbols with literals.

Motion-state transition helpers often preserve animation frame via `fp->cur_anim_frame` and use flags such as `0x0C4C508E`; matching work should preserve those exact arguments rather than abstracting them prematurely.
Ground/air collision callbacks for fighter specials commonly delegate to shared helpers (`ft_800827A0`, `ft_80081D0C`) and then call transition helpers only when the helper indicates a state change.
Keep generated object configuration and split files synchronized with source/header moves; a matching source change alone is insufficient for linking in this project.
naming_conventions: Purin/Jigglypuff files use the `ftPr_` prefix, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialLw.c`, `ftPr_SpecialS.c`, and `ftPr_Init.c`.
Grounded and aerial variants are distinguished with `SpecialLw` versus `SpecialAirLw`, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialLw_Enter` and `ftPr_SpecialAirLw_Enter`.
Standard fighter callback suffixes appear as `Enter`, `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Unknown helper routines kept address-based names such as `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D104` and `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D19C`.
Motion-state enum names in the Lw source use side-specific names such as `ftPr_MS_SpecialLwL`, `ftPr_MS_SpecialLwR`, `ftPr_MS_SpecialAirLwL`, and `ftPr_MS_SpecialAirLwR`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed the leftover assembly file from `ftPr_SpecialLw.s` to `ftPr_SpecialS.s` after removing the now-linked `0x8013CE8C..0x8013D234` Lw functions.
Used precise split ranges to make `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` own only its 936-byte text segment and `0x804D9C20..0x804D9C2C` `.sdata2` segment.
Converted `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` arguments from extern float symbols to literals: facing check `-1 == fp->facing_dir`, start frame `0`, animation rate `1`, and blend/other float `0`.
Left `ftPr_SpecialS.c` as `NonMatching` while keeping the remaining assembly object, allowing partial progress without regressing unlinked functions.
Preserved no-op IASA callbacks as empty functions/returns for matching tiny 4-byte functions.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that link `ftPr_SpecialS.c` after this split.
Search for other fighter PRs that split a combined special-move assembly file into separate logical C files.
Search for uses of direct `-1`, `0`, and `1` float literals replacing `ftPr_Init_804D9C20/24/28`-style extern constants.
Check whether address-named helpers `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D104` and `ftPr_SpecialLw_8013D19C` were later given semantic names.
